---
title: "Stock Database"
date: 2026-05-20
description: "Python, PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB, and R Shiny pipeline for collecting and visualizing minutely stock market data."
categories: ["data-engineering"]
image: candles.png
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
---

## Overview

This project is an automated Python/PostgreSQL data [pipeline](https://github.com/Ambrois/stock-data-collection) and R Shiny [dashboard](http://stockdb.ambrois.uk/) for collecting, storing, and visualizing minute-level stock market data from [Alpaca](https://alpaca.markets/).

The database holds minutely price data for the S&P 500 companies since January 2016, and updates (market) daily. It is hosted on a small local server, where the data is stored securely in a bubblewrapped hard-drive. The dashboard is minimal and designed for checking database health and data quality. 

The live dashboard is available at <https://stockdb.ambrois.uk>. *The dashboard link is .uk because .uk domains are very cheap on cloudflare. I am based in the US.*

## Tools Used

- **R Shiny** for the interactive dashboard
- **PostgreSQL and TimescaleDB** for storing time-series stock data
- **Python** for data collection scripts
- **Alpaca Market Data API** for stock price data
- **systemd** for scheduled updates and service monitoring
- **Cloudflare DNS** for keeping the public dashboard reachable

## What I Learned

This project gave me experience building and connecting several parts of a simple production-style workflow: API ingestion, database storage, scheduled jobs, a web dashboard, and deployment behind a public URL.

## Future work:

- Add websocket ingestion.
- Add options data.
